title: Pontormo, The Entombment of Christ
description: This quiz is for the video Pontormo, The Entombment of Christ.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which historical circumstances may have had an influence on the Mannerist style we see in this painting?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b85f38f6c9e4c2795db0acbdab4eb16.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The Mannerist style that we see in Pontormo's painting may be related to both the rule of the Medici in Florence as Grand Dukes (essentially as an hereditary monarchy) and also to the spiritual upheavals of the Protestant Reformation.**

choices.content: the rule of the Medici and the dissolution of the Florentine republic
the invention of the printing press

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is not true of Pontormo's *The Entombment of Christ*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b85f38f6c9e4c2795db0acbdab4eb16.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The facial expressions appear as masks, not as real people, and the setting is unrealistic. In addition, unlike the Renaissance interest in stability and balance, Mannerist compositions are often needlessly complex and lack a clear focal point.**

choices.content: Some of the figures are positioned in unnatural poses and they are set in a complex group without a clear focal point—unlike the structured pyramidal groups of the High Renaissance.
Pontormo is not interested in stability or balance, which were important artistic elements in the Renaissance.
The figures are highly stylized and there is a strong rejection of the rational spaces of Renaissance paintings.
The figures exist as real people in a realistic space created with the use of linear perspective.

assessment_items.question_content: **Mannerism expressed a different kind of spirituality than the Renaissance, which  was so heavily focused on [[☃ dropdown 1]]. Mannerism, on the other hand, was more interested in **[[☃ dropdown 2]].
hints: **Mannerist paintings often have a sense of artificiality and extreme elegance. This is different from the Renaissance  focus on naturalism.**
